text: Problem: A train runs on a circular track of radius 1500 m at 60 km/h. Find the angle turned in 10 seconds.
First, find the distance covered in 10 seconds. Convert speed to meters per second by multiplying by 5 over 18.
Now, apply s equals r times theta. The angle theta is s divided by r, which yields 1 over 9 radians.
Finally, convert 1 over 9 radians to degrees by multiplying by 180 over pi. This simplifies to 20 over pi degrees.
